publisher: Nevo Publishing Ltd. (נבו הוצאה לאור בע"מ)
historical_coverage: Site + TAU law library description: Israeli case law and legislation 'מכל הערכאות ומכל התקופות' (all courts, all periods, i.e. back to 1948); all legislative amendments ('כל תיקוני החקיקה'); Reshumot (official gazette); bills; full-text journals and books; sentencing-analysis module
metadata_availability: Rich (case numbers, courts, judges, citations) but only inside the subscriber interface; permalink system exists (FilesFolderPermalink.aspx)
api_type: none_public — no API/developer/integration page found on public site
feeds: Email mailing lists / daily legal-update newsletters (public claim on homepage)
robots_policy: Selective: homepage and some search pages crawlable; PriceList.aspx blocked by robots.txt (fetch refused ROBOTS_DISALLOWED)
terms_status: Proprietary ToS; not fully reviewed (public pages only)
license_status: Proprietary. Government bodies buy annual subscriptions via sole-supplier procurements (multiple mr.gov.il records, e.g. items 612605, 4000611335) — evidence they do negotiated org licensing
update_frequency: daily (legal-news and new-judgment sections dated current)
stability: very high (market leader, decades in operation)
legal_risk: high without license — proprietary DB, editorial enrichment layer is copyrighted
technical_difficulty: high (login wall, no public API, partial robots blocks)
recommended_ingestion: Negotiated data/content license only; approach via sales (site contact page, price list exists at /PriceList.aspx behind robots block). Do not scrape.
notes: CORPUS (their claims): deepest public claim in market — case law from all courts/all periods, full legislation with amendments, Reshumot, bills, full-text books+journals, sentencing-analysis tool. Homepage markets '90% מהשופטים... בחרו בנבו' (90% of judges/clerks/prosecutors chose Nevo) and ~33,750 subscribers. SEARCH: word/proximity/morphology/concept search publicly described. AI: no public AI-product claims found on marketing pages as of Jul 2026 — notable gap vs competitors. API: no public mention. PRICING: a public price-list page exists (PriceList.aspx) but is robots-blocked; gov procurement records confirm annual org subscriptions, sole-supplier status. LICENSING PATH: site contact page / sales; company is Nevo Hotzaa LaOr Ltd. WHITE-LABEL/EMBED: none publicly mentioned. UNKNOWNS FOR RFI: bulk-data licensing terms, API existence for partners, price list contents, whether they license raw corpus vs UI seats only, AI roadmap.

publisher: Guideline Group (קבוצת גיידליין) — contact emails are @techdin.co.il
historical_coverage: Self-described 'מאגר פסקי הדין המוביל בישראל'; own portal article claims 1.5 million judgments in the Takdin database (portal.takdin.co.il/article/Article/3720275); TAU library adds: full-text case law + legislation, pleadings, administrative/planning/antitrust decisions, AG directives, state-prosecutor guidelines, Knesset committee protocols
metadata_availability: Case metadata visible in public teaser pages (Takdin Light); full metadata behind subscription
feeds: 'Smart Agent' saved-search email alerts for new matching judgments (public feature claim)
robots_policy: Main site fetchable; portal.takdin.co.il robots.txt could not be fetched (oversized response). Historically Takdin Light pages were deliberately Google-indexed — subject of 2019 HCJ litigation about de-indexing judgments
license_status: Proprietary; Knesset sole-supplier letter names 'תקדין, דינים ועוד' as exclusive suppliers — government licensing precedent exists
stability: high (long-established; now under Guideline Group umbrella with TechDin AI)
legal_risk: high without license; company has litigated publicly over its judgment pages (privacy/de-indexing cases)
recommended_ingestion: Negotiated license via Guideline Group; contact info@techdin.co.il, tel 03-5680800
notes: CORPUS (their claims): 1.5M judgments (own portal article; date of claim unverified — may be older), legislation, articles, plus unusual add-ons: traffic-law DB and 'Neziklik' damages-parameters DB, AG directives, prosecutor guidelines, Knesset protocols. SEARCH: unified cross-corpus search, saved-search 'Smart Agent' email alerts, search history. AI: none native — AI is spun out as sister product TechDin AI (banner cross-promotion). API: no public mention. PRICING: none public on homepage; historically had Takdin Light pay-per-view single-document purchases. LICENSING PATH: info@techdin.co.il / 03-5680800, Hayarkon 5A Bnei Brak (shared with Guideline Group). WHITE-LABEL: none mentioned. UNKNOWNS: current doc counts, data-licensing willingness, relationship/ownership history (formerly C.D.I. Systems), whether Takdin Light pay-per-doc still operates, API for partners.

publisher: Guideline Group (קבוצת גיידליין) — self-described leading Israeli LegalTech/FinTech solutions group
historical_coverage: No explicit year range published; markets access to Israeli legislation, case law (פסיקה) and precedents — presumably backed by the Takdin corpus (same group), but that linkage is inferred, not stated as a document count
metadata_availability: unknown (product is chat/AI interface; citation behavior not publicly documented)
robots_policy: Homepage fetchable; terms-of-use page fetch was rate-limited during research (not a site block)
terms_status: ToS page exists at techdin.co.il/תנאי-שימוש/ — not reviewed (fetch failed); review before any partnership
hebrew_quality: excellent (Hebrew-first AI positioning: 'מערכת ה-AI המובילה בישראל')
recommended_ingestion: Not an ingestion source — a competitor/partner AI product. If partnering, contact info@techdin.co.il, 03-5680800
notes: VERIFIED: TechDin = 'Takdin AI', the Guideline Group's Hebrew legal gen-AI system (NOT a separate database company). CLAIMS: smart search over legislation/case law, 'converse with judgments', multi-document contract analysis, precedent-based strategy recommendations, brief drafting with citations. PRICING: 14-day free trial ('ל-14 ימי התנסות ללא עלות'); no price list public. API: none mentioned. KEY SIGNAL: dinimveod.co.il (the old Hashavim 'Dinim VeOd' database domain) now 302-redirects to techdin.co.il — consolidation of legacy databases under this AI brand. LICENSING PATH: info@techdin.co.il, Hayarkon 5A Bnei Brak. UNKNOWNS: underlying corpus size/provenance, hallucination/citation guarantees, enterprise pricing, partner/API program, what became of Dinim VeOd subscribers.

publisher: Historically Hashavim H.P.S. Business Information Ltd (חשבים ה.פ.ס.); domain now redirects to Guideline Group's TechDin
historical_coverage: Historically a full Israeli case-law + legislation database ('דינים') with sister verticals Kol Oved (labor) and Misim (tax) per TAU law library; Knesset sole-supplier letter treated 'תקדין, דינים ועוד' as exclusive suppliers
feeds: Historically email bulletins ('מבזק' page, Hashavim ActiveTrail newsletters)
robots_policy: n/a — dinimveod.co.il returned 302 redirect to techdin.co.il on 2026-07-11
license_status: Presumed absorbed into Guideline Group offering
legal_risk: n/a as source; historical content presumably lives inside Takdin/TechDin corpus
recommended_ingestion: None — track as market-consolidation datapoint; any licensing goes through Guideline Group
notes: STATUS: legacy top-3 database (Hashavim's 'Dinim VeOd', plus verticals Kol Oved/labor and Misim VeAsakim/tax) whose domain now redirects to TechDin AI — strong evidence of 2020s consolidation of Israeli legal databases under Guideline Group. Historic Globes coverage describes the 'Halachot vs Takdin' CD-database war of the 1990s (Dinim originated with Halachot Publishing). Government procurement records (mr.gov.il item 517901) show past state subscriptions to 'Dinim VeOd' and 'Dinim Avoda'. RFI IMPLICATION: one negotiation with Guideline Group may cover Takdin + TechDin + Dinim legacy corpus. UNKNOWNS: whether Dinim corpus was merged, current ownership chain Hashavim->Guideline, fate of Kol Oved/Misim verticals (Hashavim's hashavim.co.il still operates them separately — verify directly).

historical_coverage: About page: operating ~25 years; claims 'מאגר נתונים של LawData כולל את כלל פסקי הדין של בתי המשפט בישראל' (all judgments of Israeli courts) plus active AND repealed legislation, regulations; TAU library adds: full-text legislation+case law, legal news, pleadings, interactive form templates, calculators, case summaries
metadata_availability: Judgment pages have SEO-visible metadata (case number, court, date) on public .asp pages
feeds: Legal news section updated (July 2026 items observed)
robots_policy: Public marketing + some judgment teaser pages crawlable (classic .asp URLs indexed by Google)
terms_status: Extensive disclaimers on site re AI content provided as-is; full ToS not reviewed
technical_difficulty: medium-high (registration wall; legacy ASP stack; no API)
recommended_ingestion: Negotiated license; contact tel 03-5383229, registration page offers free signup ('הירשמו ללא עלות') for evaluation
notes: CORPUS (their claims): all Israeli court judgments, active+repealed laws, regulations, pleadings ('תיקיות משפט'), templates, calculators — differentiates on practice tools. AI: markets an AI-powered 'personal legal assistant' ('עוזר משפטי חכם מופעל בהנעת AI') plus document-analysis tools (DOCX->PDF, review) — an incumbent that has bolted on AI. SEARCH: cross-corpus search. API: none mentioned. PRICING: not public; free registration/trial. LICENSING PATH: 03-5383229 / site contact; smaller and likely more licensing-flexible than Nevo. WHITE-LABEL: none mentioned. UNKNOWNS: document counts, actual earliest-year coverage (site shows cases back to at least 2006-2009 in public pages; '25 years' claim implies ~2000), pricing tiers, data-licensing appetite, AI vendor/stack.

publisher: Legalmate (Israeli legal-AI startup; covered by Geektime/Mako 2024-2025)
historical_coverage: No public year-range or document-count claims found; markets an 'AI-based legal database' of judgments, cases and decisions (מאגר משפטי מבוסס AI | פסקי דין, תיקים והחלטות)
robots_policy: Site fetchable but heavily JS-rendered — marketing pages return mostly metadata to crawlers
terms_status: not reviewed (not reachable via static fetch)
recommended_ingestion: None — competitor/partner candidate, not a data source. Direct outreach needed via legalmate.io contact
notes: VERIFIED: real Israeli product — AI litigation-case-management system with three modules: case management, AI legal database (judgments/cases/decisions search), and an AI personal assistant 'adapted to Israeli law'. Press: Geektime piece framing it as preventing AI 'court embarrassments' (hallucinated citations); Mako coverage on cost reduction. PRICING: /pricing page exists but is client-side rendered — contents unknown; a vendr.com listing exists (US SaaS marketplace) suggesting B2B sales motion. API: none public. SEARCH/AI: natural-language AI research over Hebrew case law. UNKNOWNS (all require outreach): corpus size/source (possibly court-system scrape or licensed feed), pricing, API, company registration name, funding.

publisher: Axioma Information Solutions (acquired Cligal 2022; product founded 2017)
historical_coverage: n/a — no legal corpus of its own; syncs documents from Net HaMishpat (Israel's court e-filing system) into user case files
api_type: none_public — but has productized integrations (Net HaMishpat sync 'SyncroNet', Lizzy AI send-to button)
feeds: Court-file update sync from Net HaMishpat (subscriber feature)
robots_policy: Fully crawlable marketing site incl. pricing and KB
stability: high (claims 20,000+ lawyers incl. Ministry of Justice, Knesset, Tax Authority)
legal_risk: low as research subject; n/a as corpus
recommended_ingestion: None — not a corpus. Relevant as DISTRIBUTION/EMBED channel: its Lizzy AI integration proves it embeds third-party legal AI
notes: VERIFIED: Cligal is Israel's most popular legal PRACTICE-MANAGEMENT software ('התכנה המשפטית הפופולרית ביותר בישראל'), NOT a case-law database. PUBLIC PRICING (rare in this market): Litigation ₪74/user/mo, Real Estate ₪99, Civil-Commercial ₪116, Management ₪158 (annual billing); Cligal Management from ₪79; KYC module from ₪49; 5+ seat discounts; 14-day free trial. AI: document analysis/automation claims + a built-in 'send to Lizzy' button integrating Lizzy AI into its DMS — the clearest public embed/partnership precedent in the Israeli market and a template for a LawME integration. LICENSING/PARTNER PATH: info@cligal.com, WhatsApp 073-7773245, Raoul Wallenberg 24 TLV. UNKNOWNS: partner-program terms, technical integration mechanism (API vs plugin), exclusivity of the Lizzy deal.

historical_coverage: n/a — no published case-law corpus; it is a document-centric gen-AI workbench (draft/review/analyze/proofread), not a research database
api_type: none_public as open API, but demonstrated B2B integration surface (embedded in Cligal DMS; Microsoft Word add-in; VPC and self-hosted deployment options)
terms_status: not reviewed; strong public security claims (data never used for training, anonymization, policy enforcement)
hebrew_quality: excellent — explicitly markets 'flawless Hebrew support' with RTL generation and Hebrew menus
stability: medium (funded startup; active events/ACC Israel presence, Jan 2026 GC webinar)
recommended_ingestion: None — competitor in the AI layer, potential integration model to study. Contact via lizzyai.com contact page
notes: VERIFIED: lizzy.co.il is not the product domain — the company is LizzyAI Ltd at lizzyai.com, 'The next generation legal AI': secure gen-AI platform for legal professionals (multi-LLM access, playbooks, AI agents, private-LLM training, citations/redlines, scanned-doc handling). NOT a case-law database — no corpus claims. PRICING: usage-based 'pay as you grow', free during adoption phase; SSO on VPC/self-host tiers. ENTERPRISE SIGNALS: strongest deployment story of the new entrants (shared / VPC / self-hosted). EMBED PRECEDENT: white-label-adjacent — embedded 'send to Lizzy' inside Cligal. UNKNOWNS: whether they plan case-law research (would collide with LawME), partner API terms, pricing specifics, funding/headcount.

publisher: LawMate (Israeli legal-AI research company; product at law-mate.com)
historical_coverage: No public year-range/doc-count; claims answers 'backed by case law, legislation, regulations and legal literature' with AI that understands precedent hierarchy; Israeli-law focused with multi-language capability
stability: medium (startup, but institutional adoption: listed as a subscribed resource by Bar-Ilan University library)
recommended_ingestion: None — competitor benchmark. Outreach via site contact / trial signup
notes: VERIFIED: LawMate (law-mate.com; distinct from 'Legalmate'/legalmate.io — easy to confuse) is an AI legal-research chat for attorneys grounded in Israeli case law/legislation/literature; used by named Israeli firms and licensed by Bar-Ilan University library (academic channel signal). PUBLIC PRICING (unusual transparency): flat ₪200+VAT per user/month; volume discounts 10% (11-50 users), 20% (51-100), 25% (101+); extra 5% for annual; free trial with all modules. TARGETS judges, lawyers, in-house. API: none public. WHITE-LABEL: none mentioned. UNKNOWNS: corpus source (licensed from an incumbent? court scrape?), citation reliability guarantees, company legal name/funding.

publisher: Psakdin — independent Israeli legal portal / lawyer-marketing marketplace
primary_or_secondary: secondary (editorial rewrites of judgments) + selected primary
historical_coverage: Claims 'אלפי פסקי דין במגוון תחומים' (thousands of judgments across fields) — orders of magnitude smaller than Nevo/Takdin; strength is editorial case summaries and articles, not comprehensive coverage
auth_required: partial (much content free; registration tiers incl. lawyer membership)
license_status: Proprietary editorial content (articles are copyrighted works even where underlying judgments are public)
legal_risk: medium — editorial layer clearly copyrighted; ad/marketing-driven content may embed bias
recommended_ingestion: Not recommended as corpus (thin primary coverage, strong editorial copyright). Note their 'Halfi' AI bot ('בוט משפטי חכם שלומד מפסקי דין') claiming case-outcome prediction
notes: BUSINESS MODEL: freemium consumer portal + lawyer-marketing directory, not a professional research database; paid tiers are mainly lawyer-membership/marketing. AI: 'Halfi' bot marketed as learning from judgments to predict outcomes — public claim only, no methodology published. CORPUS: 'thousands' of judgments (self-claim) + large free article library. API/WHITE-LABEL: none mentioned. LICENSING PATH: צור קשר form. RELEVANCE TO LawME: low as data source; moderate as consumer-channel competitor and SEO benchmark. UNKNOWNS: Halfi's real capabilities, willingness to syndicate editorial summaries.

publisher: Nezikist Group Ltd. (קבוצת נזיקיסט, founded 2009, Hod HaSharon); product also at briefly.org.il
historical_coverage: Claims an AI legal database of 'over 2 million court rulings and decisions from all Israeli courts' — the largest explicit public document-count claim found in this survey (vs Takdin's 1.5M)
stability: medium (parent company since 2009; Briefly O.S. is a recent all-in-one relaunch)
recommended_ingestion: None as source; competitor to benchmark. Contact 03-5472447 ext.1, HaNagar 24 Hod HaSharon; 14-day trial no credit card
notes: VERIFIED NEW ENTRANT: 'Briefly Legal O.S.' — all-in-one AI operating system for Israeli lawyers combining a 2M+ ruling AI database (natural-language + parametric search), AI document editor, contract risk analysis, PDF suite, digital signing, case management, court-filing automation, plus verticals (medical-legal PI analysis, real-estate, tax, actuarial damages calculators). PRICING: 14-day free trial; price list page exists but rates not captured. API/WHITE-LABEL: none mentioned. KEY QUESTION FOR RFI: where do 2M+ rulings come from (court-system harvesting vs licensed feed) — directly relevant precedent for LawME's own corpus strategy. UNKNOWNS: pricing, doc-count verification, funding, citation reliability.

publisher: Unknown (small Israeli legal-AI product; self-described 'בינה מלאכותית (AI) לעורכי דין')
primary_or_secondary: secondary (AI-generated topical syntheses over case law)
historical_coverage: Unknown; public SEO pages are AI-generated topic briefs citing Israeli Supreme Court and lower-court case law (e.g. pages on jurisdiction, class actions, disciplinary appeals)
auth_required: unknown (public SEO pages open; product access unclear)
stability: low-medium (small/new; direct fetch was rate-limited during research, verified via search results only)
legal_risk: medium; AI-generated legal content of unverified accuracy
recommended_ingestion: None. Track as long-tail AI entrant; verify by direct visit/outreach
example_documents: https://www.dinrega.com/gs/... (AI topic-brief pages on jurisdiction, class actions, AI legality in courts)
notes: NEW LONG-TAIL ENTRANT (2024-2026 wave): 'Din Rega' = 'law in a moment' — AI assistant for lawyers publishing large numbers of AI-generated Hebrew topic briefs over Israeli case law as SEO landing pages. Everything else — publisher identity, corpus, pricing, API — unknown; site could not be fetched (proxy rate limit, not site block) so all attributes are from search-result snippets and indexed page titles. Requires direct verification before treating as a real competitor. UNKNOWNS: essentially all commercial attributes.

publisher: Dyoma (דְּיוֹמָא) — online opinion/essay platform on law and public affairs
historical_coverage: n/a — publishes contemporary essays (e.g. on judicial review, standing doctrine, AG powers, legal formalism), not a corpus of judgments or legislation
robots_policy: Publicly indexed; direct fetch failed on robots.txt connect timeout during research
recommended_ingestion: None for the commercial-database track. Reclassify out of F-commercial: it is a free commentary journal, not a licensable legal database — recorded here per instruction to document verification results
notes: VERIFICATION RESULT: 'דיומא' is NOT a commercial legal database. It is a Hebrew online journal of essays on law, constitutional politics and public affairs (articles on judicial activism, justiciability doctrine, standing, AG role, constitutional custom, legal formalism). Free to read; no corpus, no subscriptions observed, no licensing relevance for case-law RAG. Included only so the 'Dioma?' lead is closed with a documented answer. If secondary-literature ingestion ever matters, approach would be ordinary copyright permission from the editors.
